name: Accurate Shot
description: You are an exceptional shot and know where to shoot an enemy in order to inflict maximum damage. You deal your Accurate Shot level in extra Damage with all ranged weapons.

name: Acute Sense
description: One of your primary five senses is highly developed, allowing you to spot what others miss. You may take Perception Tests to detect normally imperceptible details with the associated sense, such as seeing an eagle beyond everyone else's eyeshot, smelling an almost odourless poison, or hearing the breath of an unmoving mouse within a wall. This is a grouped talent — choose a specific sense (Sight, Hearing, Smell, Taste, or Touch).

name: Aethyric Attunement
description: Your experience, talent or training lets you more safely manipulate the Winds of Magic. You do not suffer a Miscast if you roll a double on a successful Channel Test.

name: Alley Cat
description: You are at home in shadowy backstreets. When using Stealth (Urban), you may reverse the dice of any failed Test if this will score a Success.

name: Ambidextrous
description: You can use your off-hand far better than most folk, either by training or innate talent. You only suffer a penalty of -10 to Tests relying solely on your secondary hand, not -20. If you have this Talent twice, you suffer no penalty at all.

name: Animal Affinity
description: Wild animals feel comfortable in your presence, and often follow your lead. All creatures with the Bestial Trait not trained to be belligerent will automatically be calm in your presence unless they have a reason not to be, such as pain, an attack, being naturally hyper-aggressive, or having nearby young.

name: Arcane Magic
description: You study one of the Arcane Lores of Magic (Beasts, Death, Fire, Heavens, Metal, Shadow, Light, Life) or practice a lesser known Lore such as Hedgecraft or Necromancy. You may now memorise spells from your chosen Lore. XP cost scales with number of spells known: IntB x1 = 100 XP, IntB x2 = 200 XP, etc. You may not learn more than one Arcane Magic Lore, nor the Bless or Invoke Talents. You can unlearn this Talent for 100 XP but will immediately lose all your spells. This is a grouped talent — choose a Lore.

name: Argumentative
tests: Charm Tests when arguing and debating
description: You are used to arguing your points and winning. If you roll a successful Charm Test to debate with an opponent, you can choose to either use your rolled SL, or the number rolled on your units die. So, a successful roll of 24 could be used for +4 SL.

name: Artistic
description: You have a natural talent for art, able to produce precise sketches with nothing but time and appropriate media. Add Trade (Artist) to any Career you enter; if it is already in Career, you may instead purchase the Skill for 5 XP fewer per Advance.

name: Attractive
tests: Charm Tests to influence those attracted to you
description: When you successfully use Charm to influence those attracted to you, you can choose to either use your rolled SL, or the number rolled on your units die. So, a successful roll of 38 could be used for +8 SL.

name: Battle Rage
description: You are better able to control your Frenzy in combat. You can end your Frenzy with a successful Cool Test at the end of the round.

name: Beat Blade
description: For your Action, you can choose to Beat Blade before rolling. Perform a Melee Test; if successful, your opponent loses -1 Advantage, and loses a further -1 per SL you score. This Test is not Opposed. This Talent is of no use if your opponent has no weapon, or has a larger Size than you.

name: Beneath Notice
description: Assuming you are properly attired and not in an incongruous position, those of a higher Status Tier will normally ignore you unless your presence becomes inappropriate. Further, characters with a higher Status Tier than you gain no Advantage for striking or wounding you in combat.

name: Berserk Charge
tests: Melee on a Round when you Charge
description: You hurl yourself at your enemies with reckless abandon, using the force of your charge to add weight to your strikes. When you Charge, you gain +1 Damage to all Melee attacks per level in this Talent.

name: Blather
description: You use your Charm Skill to Blather. Attempt an Opposed Charm/Intelligence Test. Success gives your opponent a Stunned Condition. For each level you have in Blather, your opponent gains another Stunned Condition. Targets Stunned by Blather may do nothing other than stare at you dumbfounded. Once the last Stunned Condition ends, the target may not be best pleased. Should you stop talking, your opponent immediately loses all Stunned Conditions caused by your Blather. Generally, you can only attempt to Blather at a character once per scene.

name: Bless
description: You are watched over by one of the Gods and can empower simple prayers. You can now deploy the Blessings of your deity. Under normal circumstances, you may only ever know one Divine Lore for the Bless Talent. This is a grouped talent — choose a Divine Lore.

name: Bookish
description: You are as at home in a library as a seaman at sea or a farmer a-farming. When using Research, you may reverse the dice of any failed Test if this will score a success.

name: Break and Enter
tests: Melee when forcing or breaking inanimate objects
description: You are an expert at quickly breaking down doors and forcing entry. You may add +1 Damage for each level in this Talent when determining damage against inanimate objects such as windows, chests, doors, and similar.

name: Briber
description: You are an exceedingly skilled briber. The GM should reduce the base cost of any required bribe by 10% per level you have in Briber, to a minimum of 10% of the original amount.

name: Cardsharp
tests: Gamble and Sleight of Hand when playing card games
description: You are used to playing, and winning, at cards. When you successfully use Gamble or Sleight of Hand when playing cards, you can choose to either use your rolled SL, or the number rolled on your units die. If you play a real card game, you may receive extra cards per deal equal to your Cardsharp level, then discard down to the appropriate hand-size.

name: Careful Strike
description: You are skilled at hitting your enemy exactly where you want to. You may modify your Hit Location result by up to +/-10 per time you have this Talent.

name: Carouser
tests: Charm at Parties, Gossip at Parties, Consume Alcohol
description: You are a seasoned drinker and know how to party hard. You may reverse the dice of any failed Consume Alcohol Test if this will score a Success.

name: Catfall
description: You are nimble and balanced like a cat. Whenever you fall, you attempt an Athletics Test. If successful, reduce the distance fallen by 1 yard, +1 extra yard per +1 SL scored, for the purposes of calculating Damage.

name: Cat-tongued
description: When using Charm to lie, listeners do not get to oppose your Charm with their Intuition to detect if there is something fishy in what you say.

name: Chaos Magic
description: By accident or design you have lost a portion of your soul to one of the Dark Gods. Your ruinous patron immediately grants you access to a single spell from the chosen Lore and you gain a Corruption point. Each time you take this Talent (always 100 XP), you learn another spell and gain a Corruption point. Under normal circumstances, you may only ever know one Lore of Chaos Magic. Maximum equals the number of spells available in the chosen Lore. This is a grouped talent — choose a Chaos Lore (Nurgle, Slaanesh, or Tzeentch).

name: Combat Aware
description: You are used to scanning the battlefield to make snap decisions. You may take a Challenging (+0) Perception Test to ignore Surprise, which is modified by circumstance as normal.

name: Combat Master
description: Your accumulated years of combat experience allow you to keep lesser fighters at bay. For each level in this Talent, you count as one more person for the purposes of determining if one side out-numbers the other. This Talent only comes into play when you are out-numbered.

name: Combat Reflexes
description: You react like a flash of lightning. Add 10 to your Initiative for each level in this Talent when determining Combat Initiative.

name: Commanding Presence
description: Your presence fills others with hushed awe and admiration. Those with a lower Status may not resist your Leadership tests with their Willpower. Enemies are still no more likely to respect or obey you, but the common folk rarely stand against you.

name: Concoct
description: You are skilled at making potions, philtres, and draughts on the go. You may take one free Crafting Endeavour to use Trade (Apothecary) without need of a Workshop. Other Crafting Endeavours use the normal rules.

name: Contortionist
tests: Perform and Agility Tests when contorting helps
description: You can bend and manipulate your body in a myriad of seemingly unnatural ways, allowing you to squeeze through unlikely gaps and bend in crazy ways, giving benefits determined by the GM, possibly with a successful Agility test.

name: Coolheaded
description: You gain a permanent +5 bonus to your starting Willpower Characteristic (this does not count towards your Advances).

name: Crack the Whip
tests: Drive or Ride Tests when Fleeing or Running
description: You know how to get the most out of your animals. When an animal you control is Fleeing or Running, it gains +1 Movement if you are using a whip.

name: Craftsman
description: You have true creative talent. Add the associated Trade Skill to any Career you enter. If the Trade Skill is already in your Career, you may instead purchase the Skill for 5 XP fewer per Advance. This is a grouped talent — choose a Trade specialisation.

name: Criminal
description: You are an active criminal making money from illegal sources. When Earning, gain bonus money per career level (Level 1: +2d10 brass; Level 2: +1d10 silver; Level 3: +2d10 silver; Level 4: +1 gold). Others consider you lower Status unless they also have Criminal. Law enforcers are always suspicious. With GM consent, you may spend XP to remove levels of Criminal. Maximum is unlimited (represented as 99).

name: Deadeye Shot
description: You always hit an opponent right where you intended. Instead of reversing the dice to determine which Hit Location is struck with your ranged weapons, you may pick a location.

name: Dealmaker
description: You are a skilled businessman who knows how to close a deal. When using the Haggle skill, you reduce or increase the price of the products by an extra 10%.

name: Detect Artefact
tests: Intuition tests to detect magical artefacts
description: You may attempt an Intuition Test for any magical artefact touched. If successful, you sense the item is magical; each SL also provides a specific special rule the item uses. Normally, you may only attempt this Test once per artefact touched.

name: Diceman
tests: Gamble and Sleight of Hand when playing dice games
description: You are a dicing master. When you successfully use Gamble or Sleight of Hand when playing with dice, you can choose to use your rolled SL or the number rolled on your units die. If playing real-life dice games, always roll extra dice equal to your Diceman level and choose the best results.

name: Dirty Fighting
description: You have been taught all the dirty tricks of unarmed combat. You may choose to cause an extra +1 Damage for each level in Dirty Fighting with any successful Melee (Brawling) hit. Note: using this Talent will be seen as cheating in any formal bout.

name: Disarm
description: For your Action, you may attempt an Opposed Melee/Melee test. If you win, your opponent loses a held weapon, which flies 1d10 feet in a random direction. If you win by 2 SL, you can determine how far the weapon is flung; by 4 SL, choose the direction; by 6 SL or more, you can take your opponent's weapon. This Talent is of no use if your opponent has no weapon or is a larger Size than you.

name: Distract
description: You may use your Move to perform a Distraction. Resolved by an Opposed Athletics/Cool Test. If you win, your opponent can gain no Advantage until the end of the next Round.

name: Doomed
description: At the age of 10, a Priest of Morr foretold your death in an incense-laden ritual called the Dooming. In conjunction with your GM, come up with a suitable Dooming. Should your character die in a fashion that matches your Dooming, your next character gains a bonus of half the total XP your dead character accrued during play.

name: Drilled
tests: Melee Tests when beside an ally with Drilled
description: You have been trained to fight shoulder-to-shoulder with other soldiers. If an enemy causes you to lose Advantage when standing beside an active ally with the Drilled Talent, you may keep 1 lost Advantage for each time you have taken the Drilled Talent.

name: Dual Wielder
tests: Melee or Ranged when attacking with two weapons
description: When armed with two weapons, you may attack with both for your Action. Roll to hit with the primary hand weapon. If you hit, the secondary weapon uses the same dice roll reversed. The second attack is Opposed with a new defending roll. If you choose to attack with both weapons, all defensive rolls until the start of your next Turn suffer a penalty of -10. You do not gain Advantage when Dual Wielding unless both attacks hit.

name: Embezzle
description: Whenever you secure money when Earning, you may attempt an Opposed Intelligence Test with your employer. If you win, you skim 2d10+SL brass pennies, 1d10+SL silver shillings, or 1+SL gold crowns without being detected. If your employer wins by 6+ SL, the embezzling is detected.

name: Enclosed Fighter
description: You have learned to make the most benefit out of fighting in enclosed spaces. You ignore penalties to Melee caused by confined spaces such as tunnels, the frontline, small fighting pits, and similar, and can use the Dodge Skill even if it would normally be disallowed due to lack of space.

name: Etiquette
description: You can blend in socially with the chosen group so long as you are dressed and acting appropriately. If you do not have the Talent, those with it will note your discomfort in the unfamiliar environment. This is a grouped talent — choose a social group (e.g. Criminals, Cultists, Guilders, Nobles, Scholars, Servants, Soldiers).

name: Fast Hands
tests: Sleight of Hand, Melee (Brawling) to touch an opponent
description: Bystanders get no passive Perception Tests to spot your use of the Sleight of Hand Skill; they can only Oppose your Sleight of Hand Tests if they actively suspect your movements. Further, attempts to use Melee (Brawling) to simply touch an opponent gain a bonus of +10 x your level in Fast Hands.

name: Fast Shot
tests: Ranged when making a Fast Shot
description: If you have a loaded ranged weapon, you can fire it outside the normal Initiative Order before any other combatant reacts in the following Round. Employing Fast Shot requires both your Action and Move for your upcoming turn. If two or more characters use Fast Shot, the character who has taken this Talent most goes first.

name: Fearless
tests: Cool to oppose your Enemy's Intimidate, Fear, and Terror
description: With a single Average (+20%) Cool Test, you may ignore any Intimidate, Fear, or Terror effects from the specified enemy when encountered. This is a grouped talent — choose an enemy type (e.g. Beastmen, Greenskins, Outlaws, Vampires, Watchmen, Witches).

name: Feint
description: You may make a Feint for your Action against any opponent using a weapon. Resolved with an Opposed Melee (Fencing)/Melee Test. If you win, and you attack the same opponent before the end of the next Round, you may add the SL of your Feint to your attack roll.

name: Field Dressing
description: If you fail a Heal Test when using Bandages, you may reverse the result if this will score a success; however, if you do so, you may not score more than +1 SL as you focus on speed over accuracy.

name: Fisherman
description: Assuming a large enough body of water is available, you are automatically assumed to be able to fish enough to feed yourself and a number of others equal to your level in Fisherman, assuming you spend at least an hour or so with a line and bait. You may secure more fish using the normal foraging rules.

name: Flagellant
tests: Any for resisting the Ruinous Powers
description: Each day, you must spend half a bell praying as you maintain a number of Wounds suffered equal to your level in Flagellant. Until you next sleep, if you have the Frenzy Talent you may enter Frenzy immediately without testing. The Frenzy Talent is added to any career you are in. Should you fail to flagellate yourself on any given day, or allow your flesh to be healed, you may not spend any Resilience or Resolve until you flagellate again.

name: Flee!
description: When your life is on the line you are capable of impressive bursts of speed. Your Movement Attribute counts as 1 higher when Fleeing.

name: Fleet Footed
description: You gain +1 to your Movement Attribute.

name: Frenzy
description: You can Frenzy as described in the combat rules.

name: Frightening
description: Anyone sane thinks twice before approaching you. If you wish, you have a Fear Rating of 1. Add +1 to this number per extra time you have this Talent.

name: Furious Assault
description: Once per Round, if you hit an opponent in close combat, you may immediately spend an Advantage or your Move to make an extra attack (assuming you have your Move remaining).

name: Gregarious
description: You just like talking to other folk and it seems they like talking to you. You may reverse any failed Gossip Test if this allows the Test to succeed.

name: Gunner
description: You can reload blackpowder weapons with practiced ease. Add SL equal to your level in Gunner to any Extended Test to reload a Blackpowder weapon.

name: Hardy
description: You gain a permanent addition to your Wounds, equal to your Toughness Bonus. If your Toughness Bonus should increase, then the number of Wounds Hardy provides also increases.

name: Hatred
description: You are consumed with hatred for something in the Old World. Each time you take this Talent you develop hatred for a new group. This is a grouped talent — choose a group (e.g. Beastmen, Greenskins, Monsters, Outlaws, Sigmarites, Undead, Witches).

name: Holy Hatred
description: Your prayers drip with the hatred you feel for your blasphemous enemies. You deal +1 Damage with Miracles for each level in this Talent.

name: Holy Visions
tests: Intuition Tests when on holy ground
description: You automatically know when you enter Holy Ground, and may take an Intuition Test to receive visions regarding the local area if significant events have occurred there in the past.

name: Hunter's Eye
tests: Any Test to trail or capture game
description: When travelling through well-stocked lands, you are automatically assumed to be able to hunt down enough game to feed yourself and a number of others equal to your level in Hunter's Eye, so long as you have time and the correct equipment.

name: Impassioned Zeal
tests: Charm when speaking about your cause
description: When you talk about your cause, case, or religion, your words fill with passion and fervent zeal. You may double your Fellowship for the purposes of determining the number of people influenced by your Public Speaking when talking about your cause.

name: Implacable
description: It takes a lot to finish you off. You can ignore the Wound loss from a Bleeding Condition. Each level in this Talent lets you ignore the Wound loss from an extra Bleeding Condition.

name: In-fighter
tests: Melee when in-fighting, or to enter in-fighting
description: You are skilled at drawing in close to an opponent. You suffer no penalties for fighting against an opponent with a longer weapon than you. Further, if you use the optional rules for In-fighting, gain a bonus of +10 to hit your opponent.

name: Inspiring
description: Your rousing words and pleas can turn the tide of a battle. Multiplies the number of soldiers you can influence with Leadership during war: x5 at level 1, x10 at 2, x20 at 3, x50 at 4, x100 at 5, x200 at 6, x500 at 7, x1000 at 8, all who can hear at 9.

name: Instinctive Diction
description: You instinctively understand the language of Magick. You do not suffer a Miscast if you roll a double on a successful Language (Magick) Test.

name: Invoke
description: You are blessed by one of the Gods and can empower one of your Cult's Miracles. You may purchase extra miracles for 100 XP per miracle you currently know. Under normal circumstances, you may not learn more than one Invoke Talent. You may not learn the Petty Magic or Arcane Magic Talents. You can unlearn this Talent for 100 XP but will lose all miracles. This is a grouped talent — choose a Divine Lore.

name: Iron Jaw
description: Whenever you gain one or more Stunned Conditions, you may make an immediate Challenging (+0) Endurance Test to not take one of them, with each SL removing an extra Stunned Condition.

name: Iron Will
description: Use of the Intimidate skill does not cause Fear in you, and will not stop you speaking out against the intimidating party.

name: Jump Up
description: You may perform a Challenging (+0) Agility Test to immediately regain your feet whenever you gain a Prone Condition. For every +10 Strength the blow has over your Toughness, you suffer a penalty of -10 to the Test, and vice versa.

name: Kingpin
description: You have earned an air of respectability despite your nefarious ways. You may ignore the Status loss of the Criminal Talent.

name: Lightning Reflexes
description: You gain a permanent +5 bonus to your starting Agility Characteristic (this does not count towards your Advances).

name: Linguistics
description: You have a natural affinity for languages. Given a month's exposure to any Language, you count the associated Language Skill as a Basic Skill with a successful Intelligence Test (which can be attempted once per month). Linguistics does not work with Language (Magick).

name: Lip Reading
description: You can tell what people are saying by simply watching their lips. If you have an unobstructed view of the speaker's lower face, you can attempt a Perception Test to understand what they are saying.

name: Luck
description: Your maximum Fortune Points now equal your current Fate points plus the number of times you have taken Luck.

name: Magical Sense
description: You may attempt an Average (+20) Intuition Test whenever you encounter a spellcaster. If you pass, you sense the target is a witch. If you score an Astounding Success (+6 SL), you can also determine the target's highest Channelling Specialisation.

name: Magic Resistance
description: You are resistant to magic. You must Oppose any incoming spell with your Willpower, just as if you were a spellcaster using Language (Magick) to dispel. You may never learn the Arcane Magic, Bless, Invoke, Petty Magic, or Witch! Talents.

name: Magnum Opus
description: You are an undisputed master in your field. Each time you take this Talent you may create a single extraordinary work of art with one of your Art or Trade Skills. Selling the piece will net you at least ten times its normal value. Maximum is unlimited (represented as 99).

name: Marksman
description: You gain a permanent +5 bonus to your starting Ballistic Skill (this does not count towards your Advances).

name: Master of Disguise
description: You are an expert at taking on the appearance and mannerisms of others. With nothing but posture changes, face twisting, and careful use of appropriate clothing, you no longer look like yourself without having to use a Disguise Kit.

name: Master Orator
description: You are skilled at firing up crowds. You gain a SL bonus equal to your levels of Master Orator to any Charm Test when Public Speaking before a crowd.

name: Master Tradesman
description: You are exceptionally skilled at your specified Trade skill. You reduce the required SL of any Extended Test using your Trade Skill by the level of your Master Tradesman Talent. This is a grouped talent — choose a Trade specialisation.

name: Menacing
description: You have an imposing presence. When using the Intimidate Skill, gain a SL bonus equal to your levels of Menacing.

name: Mimic
tests: Entertain (Acting) Tests where accents are important
description: You may replicate any accent you are exposed to for at least a day with an Initiative Test (once per day). Once passed, you may always mimic the accent and locals will believe you to be one of their own.

name: Night Vision
description: Assuming you have at least a faint source of light, you can see clearly for 20 yards per level of Night Vision. Further, you can extend the effective illumination distance of any light sources by 20 yards per level of Night Vision.

name: Nimble Fingered
description: You gain a permanent +5 bonus to your starting Dexterity (this does not count towards your Advances).

name: Noble Blood
tests: Any Test influenced by your Status
description: You are either born into the nobility, or otherwise elevated to it. Assuming you are dressed appropriately, you are always considered of higher Status than others unless they also have the Noble Blood Talent, where Status is compared as normal.

name: Nose for Trouble
description: You may attempt an Intuition Test to spot those seeking to cause trouble or harm, even if normally you would not be allowed a Test. If any troublemakers you spot start combat, you may ignore any Surprised Condition they would normally inflict.

name: Numismatics
tests: Evaluate to establish the worth of coins
description: You can judge the true value of a coin by experience alone, not even requiring a Test. Further, you can identify forged coins with a Simple Evaluate Test; it is never Opposed by the SL of the Forger.

name: Old Salt
description: You are an experienced seaman. You can ignore all negative modifiers to Tests at sea derived from poor weather, rolling ships, and similar. Further, you count as two seamen towards the minimum crew to pilot a sea-going vessel.

name: Orientation
description: You have an instinctual feel for direction. You automatically know which direction is north with a glimpse at the stars, trees, or whatever other signs you are familiar with.

name: Panhandle
description: You are a skilled beggar. You can perform a Charm Test every half hour when Begging, not every hour.

name: Perfect Pitch
tests: Entertain (Sing), Language (Tonal Languages such as Eltharin, Cathayan, and Magick)
description: You have perfect pitch, able to replicate notes perfectly and identify them without even making a Test. Add Entertain (Sing) to any Career you enter; if already in your Career, you may instead purchase the Skill for 5 XP fewer per Advance.

name: Petty Magic
description: You have the spark to cast magic. When you take this Talent, you permanently memorise a number of spells equal to your Willpower Bonus. You can learn extra Petty spells at escalating XP cost: WPB x1 = 50 XP, WPB x2 = 100 XP, WPB x3 = 150 XP, etc.

name: Pharmacist
description: You are highly skilled at pharmacy. You may reverse any failed Trade (Apothecary) test if this allows the Test to succeed.

name: Pilot
tests: Row or Sail Tests while navigating unsure waters
description: If you fail a Test to pass through dangerous waters, you may reverse the result if it will score a success; however, you may not score more than +1 SL as you catch the incoming danger at the last moment.

name: Public Speaker
description: You are a skilled orator and know how to work large groups of people. Multiplies the number of people you can influence with your Charm Skill when Public Speaking: x5 at level 1, x10 at 2, x20 at 3, x50 at 4, x100 at 5, x200 at 6, x500 at 7, x1000 at 8, all who can hear at 9.

name: Pure Soul
description: Your soul is pure, quite resistant to the depredations of Chaos. You may gain extra Corruption points equal to your level of Pure Soul before having to Test to see if you become corrupt.

name: Rapid Reload
description: You can reload ranged weapons with practiced ease. You add SL equal to your level in Rapid Reload to any Test to reload a ranged weapon.

name: Reaction Strike
description: When you are Charged, you may attempt a Challenging (+0) Initiative Test to gain an immediate Free Attack outside the normal turn sequence. You may make as many Reaction Strikes in a Round as you have levels in this Talent, but can only attack each individual charger once each.

name: Read/Write
description: You are one of the rare literate individuals in the Old World. You are assumed to be able to read and write (if appropriate) all of the Languages you can speak.

name: Relentless
description: When you have your mind set on a target, there is nothing anyone can do to stop you reaching them. If you use Advantage when Disengaging, you may keep a number of Advantage equal to your level of Relentless. Further, you may use Advantage to Disengage even if you have lower Advantage than your opponents.

name: Resistance
tests: All those to resist the associated Threat
description: Your strong constitution allows you to more readily survive a specific threat. You may automatically pass the first Test to resist the specified threat every session. If SL is important, use your Toughness Bonus as SL for the Test. This is a grouped talent — choose a threat type (e.g. Magic, Poison, Disease, Mutation).

name: Resolute
description: You launch into attacks with grim determination. Add your level of Resolute to your Strength Bonus when you Charge.

name: Reversal
description: If you win an Opposed Melee Test, instead of gaining +1 Advantage, you may take all your opponent's Current Advantage. If you do this, you do not cause any Damage, even if it is your Turn in the Round.

name: Riposte
description: If your weapon has the Fast quality, you may cause Damage when you are attacked, just as if it was your Action. You can Riposte a number of attacks per round equal to your Riposte level.

name: River Guide
tests: Any Lore Test concerning river matters
description: You don't need to Test for passing through dangerous stretches of water until the Difficulty for doing so is -10 or lower. Further, if you have the appropriate Lore (Local) Skill, you need never Test for navigating dangerous waters.

name: Robust
description: You are as tough as old boots and just soak up damage. You reduce all incoming Damage by an extra +1 per time you have taken the Robust Talent, even if the Damage cannot normally be reduced, but still suffer a minimum of 1 Wound from any Damage source.

name: Roughrider
description: You are at home in the saddle in even the most difficult of circumstances. Assuming you have the Ride skill, you can direct your mount to take an Action, not just a Move, without a Ride test.

name: Rover
tests: Stealth Tests in a Rural environment
description: When using Stealth in a rural environment, bystanders do not get passive Perception Tests to detect you; they can only spot you if they are specifically on look-out, or watching for hidden spies.

name: Savant
description: You are exceptionally learned. You automatically know a number of pieces of correct information equal to your Savant level about a relevant issue without having to test your Lore Skill. This is a grouped talent — choose a Lore specialisation.

name: Savvy
description: You gain a permanent +5 bonus to your starting Intelligence Characteristic (this does not count towards your Advances).

name: Scale Sheer Surface
description: You can attempt to climb even seemingly impossible surfaces such as sheer fortifications, ice shelves, plastered walls, and similar, and you ignore any penalties to Climb Tests derived from the difficulty of the surface climbed.

name: Schemer
description: Once per session, you may ask the GM one question regarding a political situation or entangled web of social connections; the GM will perform a secret Intelligence Test and provide you some observations based upon your SL.

name: Sea Legs
tests: All those taken to resist Sea Sickness
description: Under normal conditions at sea, you need never Test to see if you become Sea Sick. At other times (such as a storm, or a magically induced bout of Sea Sickness), you can ignore any penalties to Tests to avoid Sea Sickness.

name: Seasoned Traveller
tests: Any Lore Test concerning local detail
description: Add Lore (Local) to any Career you enter; if it is already in Career, you may purchase the Skill for 5 XP fewer per Advance. Can be taken twice, with a different Speciality each time (such as Altdorf, Vorbergland, or Ubersreik).

name: Second Sight
tests: Any Test to detect the Winds of Magic
description: You can perceive the shifting Winds of Magic that course from the Chaos Gates at the poles of the world. You now have the Sight.

name: Secret Identity
tests: Entertain (Acting) Tests to support your secret identities
description: You maintain a secret identity that allows you to appear richer, or perhaps poorer, than you actually are. With GM permission, choose any one Career. You may use the Social Status of the chosen Career you masquerade as rather than your own. You may create a new Secret Identity for each level.

name: Shadow
description: You may use the Shadowing rules without doing a Combined Test. Instead you test against just your Perception or your Stealth Skill, whichever is higher.

name: Sharp
description: You gain a permanent +5 bonus to your starting Initiative Characteristic (this does not count towards your Advances).

name: Sharpshooter
description: You can make aimed shots of exceptional accuracy. You ignore any negative Difficulty modifiers to Ranged Tests due to the size of your target.

name: Shieldsman
tests: Any Test to defend with a shield
description: When using a Shield to defend, you gain Advantage equal to the number of levels you have in Shieldsman if you lose the Opposed Test.

name: Sixth Sense
tests: Intuition Tests involving your Sixth Sense
description: The GM may warn you if you are walking into danger; this will normally come after a secret Intuition Test on your behalf. Further, you may ignore Surprise if you pass an Intuition Test.

name: Slayer
description: When determining Damage use your opponent's Toughness Bonus as your Strength Bonus if it is higher. Further, if your target is larger than you and you score a Critical, multiply all melee Damage by the number of steps larger your target is. This multiplication is calculated after all modifiers are applied.

name: Small
description: You are much shorter than most folk in the Old World. The full rules for different Sizes are found in the Bestiary.

name: Sniper
description: Distance is of no import to your shooting skills. You suffer no penalties for shooting at Long range, and half the penalties for Extreme range.

name: Speedreader
tests: Research and Language Tests where speed of reading is important
description: You read books at a voracious pace. You may reverse a failed Research Test if this will grant success. If the speed at which you read is important during combat, a successful Language Test lets you read and fully comprehend a number of pages per Round equal to your SL plus Speedreader level.

name: Sprinter
description: You are a swift runner. Your Movement Attribute counts as 1 higher when Running.

name: Step Aside
tests: Dodge Tests to activate this Talent
description: If you use Dodge to defend against an incoming attack and win the Opposed Test, you may move up to 2 yards as you dive away, and no longer count as Engaged. None of your opponents will gain a Free Attack when you do this.

name: Stone Soup
description: You can subsist on half the amount of food required without any negative penalties, and need only test for Starvation every 3 days, not 2.

name: Stout-hearted
tests: Cool Tests to remove Broken Conditions
description: You may attempt a Cool Test to remove a Broken Condition at the end of each of your Turns as well as at the end of the Round.

name: Strider
tests: Athletics Tests to traverse the Terrain
description: You are experienced in traversing difficult ground. You ignore all movement penalties when crossing over or through a specified terrain. This is a grouped talent — choose a terrain type (e.g. Coastal, Deserts, Marshes, Rocky, Tundra, Woodlands).

name: Strike Mighty Blow
description: You know how to hit hard! You deal your level of Strike Mighty Blow in extra Damage with melee weapons.

name: Strike to Injure
description: You are an expert at striking your enemies' most vulnerable areas. You inflict your level of Strike to Injure in additional Wounds when you cause a Critical Wound.

name: Strike to Stun
tests: Melee Tests when Striking to Stun
description: You ignore the Called Shot penalty to strike the Head Hit Location when using a melee weapon with the Pummel Quality. Further, you count all improvised weapons as having the Pummel Quality.

name: Strong Back
description: You may add your levels in Strong Back to your SL in any Opposed Strength Tests, and can carry additional Encumbrance points of trappings equal to your level of Strong Back.

name: Strong Legs
description: You have strong legs able to carry you great distances when you jump. Add your Strong Legs level to your SL in any Athletics Tests involving Leaping.

name: Strong-minded
description: You are the epitome of determination and resolve. Add your level in Strong Minded to your maximum Resolve pool.

name: Strong Swimmer
description: You are an especially strong swimmer and used to holding your breath for a long time underwater. Gain a bonus of your level in Strong Swimmer to your Toughness Bonus for the purposes of holding your breath.

name: Sturdy
description: You have a brawny physique, or are very used to carrying things. Increase the number of Encumbrance Points you can carry by your Sturdy level x 2.

name: Suave
description: You gain a permanent +5 bonus to your starting Fellowship Characteristic (this does not count towards your Advances).

name: Super Numerate
description: You have a gift for calculation and can work out the solution to most mathematical problems with ease. You may use a simple calculator to represent what your PC is capable of mentally computing.

name: Supportive
tests: Social Tests to influence a superior
description: When you successfully use a social Skill to influence those with a higher Status tier, you can choose to either use your rolled SL, or the number rolled on your units die.

name: Sure Shot
description: You know how to find the weak spots in a target's armour. When you hit a target with a Ranged weapon, you may ignore Armour Points equal to your Sure Shot level.

name: Surgery
description: You can treat any Critical Wound marked as needing Surgery. You can also perform surgery to resolve internal issues with an Extended Challenging (+0) Heal Test. This will cause 1d10 Wounds and 1 Bleeding Condition per Test. After surgery, the patient must pass an Average (+20) Endurance Test or gain a Minor Infection.

name: Tenacious
description: You can double the length of time successful Endurance Tests allow you to endure a hardship, including enduring prolonged riding, exposure, rituals, and similar adversities.

name: Tinker
tests: Trade Tests to repair broken items
description: You are somewhat of a Johann-of-all-trades, able to repair almost anything. You count all non-magical Trade Skills as Basic when repairing broken items.

name: Tower of Memories
description: A recollection technique that allows you to perfectly recall a sequence of facts stored in an imaginary spire. You can recall a sequence as long as your Intelligence without a Test. For every 10 more items, increasingly difficult Intelligence Tests apply. Each time you take this Talent you may recall an extra sequence. Adds +20 to +60 bonus to Gamble Tests and other applicable Tests as determined by the GM.

name: Trapper
tests: Perception Tests to spot traps, Set Trap
description: You may take a Perception Test to spot traps automatically without having to tell the GM of your intention. The GM may prefer to make some of these Tests in private.

name: Trick Riding
description: You can use any of your Performer Skills and unmodified Dodge skill when on horseback. Further, when mounted, you can make your Move at the start of the Round instead of on your Turn.

name: Tunnel Rat
description: When using Stealth in an underground environment, bystanders do not get passive Perception Tests to detect you; they can only spot you if they are specifically on look-out, or watching for hidden others.

name: Unshakable
tests: Cool Tests to resist Blackpowder panic
description: You need only take a Cool Test to resist a Broken Condition if you are successfully wounded by a Blackpowder weapon, not just if you are shot at.

name: Very Resilient
description: You gain a permanent +5 bonus to your starting Toughness Characteristic (this does not count towards your Advances).

name: Very Strong
description: You gain a permanent +5 bonus to your starting Strength Characteristic (this does not count towards your Advances).

name: War Leader
description: Your stern gaze and inspiring words motivate your soldiers to fight on to the end. All subordinates able to see you may add your level in War Leader to their SL in one Willpower Test per Round. This bonus does not stack.

name: War Wizard
description: You are trained to cast magic while in the thick of combat. On your Turn, you may cast one Spell with a Casting Number of 5 or less for free without using your Action. However, if you do this, you may not cast another spell this Turn.

name: Warrior Born
description: You gain a permanent +5 bonus to your starting Weapon Skill Characteristic (this does not count towards your Advances).

name: Waterman
description: You are an experienced freshwater sailor. You can ignore all negatives to your Tests when onboard a barge derived from rolling waters, swaying vessels, unsure footing. Further, you count as two boatmen towards the minimum crew to pilot a river vessel.

name: Wealthy
description: You are fabulously wealthy. When Earning (including Income Endeavours) you secure +1 GC per time you have this Talent. Maximum is unlimited (represented as 99).

name: Well-prepared
description: A number of times per session equal to your level of Well-Prepared, you may pull the trapping required for the current situation from your backpack as long as it is Encumbrance 0, could feasibly have been bought recently, and does not stretch credibility too far. Whenever you do this, you must deduct the cost from your purse.

name: Witch!
description: You are a self-taught spellcaster. Add Language (Magick) to any Career you enter; if it is already in your Career, you may instead purchase the Skill for 5 XP fewer per Advance. Further, for the cost of a Resilience point, you may manifest a spell from any Arcane Lore. You can do this a number of times equal to your level in this Talent.
